title: Hollywood Comes Calling
description: Ryan Reynolds and Rob McElhenney announce their interest in buying Wrexham AFC, sending shockwaves through the football world.

title: The Trust Says Yes
description: The Wrexham Supporters Trust votes 98.6% in favour of the takeover. A new era begins.

title: Takeover Complete
description: Reynolds and McElhenney officially complete their purchase of Wrexham AFC. The new era begins.

title: Phil Parkinson Appointed
description: Experienced manager Phil Parkinson is appointed as Wrexham boss, bringing EFL know-how to the National League.

title: Paul Mullin Signs
description: League Two top scorer Paul Mullin turns down EFL offers to sign for non-league Wrexham, signalling the club's ambition.

title: Ollie Palmer Arrives
description: Ollie Palmer signs from AFC Wimbledon for a reported club-record fee, going on to score 16 goals in 26 games.

title: Playoff Heartbreak
description: After finishing 2nd in the National League, Wrexham lose a dramatic single-leg playoff semi-final to Grimsby Town 5-4 after extra time, with Luke Waterfall scoring a 119th-minute winner.

title: The Racecourse Comes Home
description: The club purchases the Racecourse Ground from Wrexham Glyndŵr University, securing the stadium's future.

title: The Documentary Premieres
description: The hit documentary series about the club premieres on FX, bringing global attention to Wrexham and the town.

title: A Royal Visit
description: King Charles III and Queen Consort Camilla visit Wrexham and the club, further raising the profile of the town.

title: FA Cup Giant-Killing
description: Wrexham beat Championship side Coventry City 4-3 in the FA Cup Third Round, then drew 3-3 with Sheffield United in a thrilling Fourth Round tie before losing the replay 3-1 at Bramall Lane.

title: Ben Foster Signs
description: Former England goalkeeper Ben Foster comes out of retirement at 39 to sign for Wrexham, bringing Premier League experience to the fifth tier.

title: National League Champions!
description: Wrexham beat Boreham Wood 3-1 to clinch the National League title with a record 111 points, ending a 15-year exile from the EFL. Paul Mullin scores 38 league goals.

title: Back-to-Back Promotion!
description: Wrexham thrash Forest Green Rovers 6-0 to clinch automatic promotion to League One. Two promotions in two seasons — the dream keeps growing.

title: Valued at £100 Million
description: New investors acquire a minority stake in the club, reportedly valuing Wrexham AFC at around £100 million — up from an estimated £2 million at the time of takeover.

title: Three in a Row — Championship Bound!
description: Wrexham beat Charlton Athletic 3-0 to seal promotion to the Championship — becoming the first team ever to achieve three consecutive promotions in England's top five divisions.
